response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
I've been spending several hours every day scrolling through social media, mostly keeping up with what's happening in the world and staying connected with friends. As a curious person who is retired and lives here in Washington DC now after being an immigrant from India, I can see how fake content could really mess things up for someone like me who isn't quite as digitally savvy as younger folks might be.

From my perspective watching this unfold over time, it seems important to treat social media a bit more cautiously than perhaps some people do. With all these high scores on the Big Five traits - openness, conscientiousness, extraversion and agreeableness are particularly strong for me at 89%,73% and 81%, with low neuroticism I tend not worry too much but still find myself drawn into drama sometimes.

I think managing fake content boils down to education first. Since my parents have different levels of education - father had a master's degree while mother only lower secondary - they didn't really keep me grounded in media literacy when we moved here decades ago. Now that I'm retired and spending so much time online, it feels like there should be more emphasis on learning how to spot misinformation.

Practically speaking, one thing that helps is following fewer accounts initially until you learn what works best for your peace of mind. Another tip would be bookmarking reliable sources instead of constantly checking everything labeled 'breaking news'. I also find turning off suggestions and notifications reduces some exposure.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I derived my opinion from personal experience with social media use, considering the high agreeableness trait which makes me more trusting initially but now leads to frustration when encountering fake content. Also drawing on curiosity (high openness) that drives interest in topics while being a retired immigrant affecting time spent and reliance on less reliable sources due to lower digital literacy.
response_parsed.tone: Reflective yet pragmatic, with an underlying sense of wisdom from lived experience
